output: Batch 3 executed. T4 and T8 are complete, and SC4 and SC8 were acknowledged.
commands_run:
node --import tsx -e "import React from 'react'; import { act } from 'react'; import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; import { JSDOM } from 'jsdom'; import { useExternalDrop } from './src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts'; const dom = new JSDOM('
'); globalThis.window = dom.window; globalThis.document = dom.window.document; globalThis.HTMLElement = dom.window.HTMLElement; globalThis.navigator = dom.window.navigator; let handlers; const applyCalls = []; const dataRef = { current: { tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }], rows: [{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-0', start: 0, end: 10, effectId: 'effect-clip-0' }] }], meta: { 'clip-0': { track: 'V1', clipType: 'hold', hold: 10 } }, clipOrder: { V1: ['clip-0'] }, registry: { assets: {} } } }; const coordinator = { editAreaRef: { current: null }, lastPosition: { isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'V1', time: 2 }, update: () => ({ isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'V1', time: 2 }), end: () => {} }; const Test = () => { handlers = useExternalDrop({ dataRef, scale: 1, scaleWidth: 100, selectedTrackId: null, applyTimelineEdit: (...args) => applyCalls.push(args), patchRegistry: () => {}, registerAsset: () => {}, uploadAsset: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); }, invalidateAssetRegistry: async () => {}, resolveAssetUrl: async (file) => file, coordinator, registerGenerationAsset: () => null, uploadImageGeneration: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); }, handleAssetDrop: () => {}, handleAddTextAt: undefined }); return null; }; const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('root')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(Test)); }); const event = { preventDefault() {}, currentTarget: { dataset: {} }, dataTransfer: { types: ['effect-layer'], files: [], getData: () => '' }, clientX: 0, clientY: 0 }; await act(async () => { await handlers.onTimelineDrop(event); }); const [rows, metaUpdates, _removed, clipOrder] = applyCalls[0]; const created = rows[0].actions.find((action) => action.id !== 'clip-0'); console.log(JSON.stringify({ applyCalls: applyCalls.length, createdTrack: metaUpdates[created.id].track, createdType: metaUpdates[created.id].clipType, createdHold: metaUpdates[created.id].hold, createdStart: created.start, createdEnd: created.end, clipOrder: clipOrder.V1 })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

task_updates.executor_notes: Restructured `TimelineRenderer` to partition regular clips from effect-layer clips, iterate visual tracks bottom-to-top, and wrap accumulated lower-track content with `EffectLayerSequence` instances from higher tracks. Verified the render path structure in diff/code inspection and confirmed the repository build passes.

Extended external drag handling for `effect-layer` drops, forced visual-track resolution/creation, created the new clip via `createEffectLayerClipMeta()`, and explicitly routed the new action through `resolveOverlaps()` before commit. Verified with a jsdom hook probe that dropping onto an occupied range created an `effect-layer` clip on `V1` and shifted it from `2s` to `10s`, confirming overlap handling ran.
commands_run:
node --import tsx -e "import React from 'react'; import { act } from 'react'; import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; import { JSDOM } from 'jsdom'; import { useExternalDrop } from './src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts'; const dom = new JSDOM('
'); globalThis.window = dom.window; globalThis.document = dom.window.document; globalThis.HTMLElement = dom.window.HTMLElement; globalThis.navigator = dom.window.navigator; let handlers; const applyCalls = []; const dataRef = { current: { tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }], rows: [{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-0', start: 0, end: 10, effectId: 'effect-clip-0' }] }], meta: { 'clip-0': { track: 'V1', clipType: 'hold', hold: 10 } }, clipOrder: { V1: ['clip-0'] }, registry: { assets: {} } } }; const coordinator = { editAreaRef: { current: null }, lastPosition: { isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'V1', time: 2 }, update: () => ({ isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'V1', time: 2 }), end: () => {} }; const Test = () => { handlers = useExternalDrop({ dataRef, scale: 1, scaleWidth: 100, selectedTrackId: null, applyTimelineEdit: (...args) => applyCalls.push(args), patchRegistry: () => {}, registerAsset: () => {}, uploadAsset: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); }, invalidateAssetRegistry: async () => {}, resolveAssetUrl: async (file) => file, coordinator, registerGenerationAsset: () => null, uploadImageGeneration: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); }, handleAssetDrop: () => {}, handleAddTextAt: undefined }); return null; }; const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('root')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(Test)); }); const event = { preventDefault() {}, currentTarget: { dataset: {} }, dataTransfer: { types: ['effect-layer'], files: [], getData: () => '' }, clientX: 0, clientY: 0 }; await act(async () => { await handlers.onTimelineDrop(event); }); const [rows, metaUpdates, _removed, clipOrder] = applyCalls[0]; const created = rows[0].actions.find((action) => action.id !== 'clip-0'); console.log(JSON.stringify({ applyCalls: applyCalls.length, createdTrack: metaUpdates[created.id].track, createdType: metaUpdates[created.id].clipType, createdHold: metaUpdates[created.id].hold, createdStart: created.start, createdEnd: created.end, clipOrder: clipOrder.V1 })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified structurally that `TimelineRenderer` now separates effect-layer clips, iterates visual tracks bottom-to-top, and wraps previously accumulated lower-track content with `EffectLayerSequence` from higher tracks. This matches the intended time-bounded effect application and track-order composition model, and the full build passed.

Confirmed with a jsdom hook probe that an `effect-layer` drop produces a clip on a visual track and routes through `resolveOverlaps()`: a drop at `2s` onto an occupied `V1` lane created an `effect-layer` clip shifted to `10s..15s` with clip order updated to include the new clip.
